definition:
1. to take away from beside, withdraw, remove , (Euripides):—; with genitive partitive to take away part of  a thing, (Euripides):—;Pass., παρῃρημένοι τὰ ὅπλα having  their arms taken from them, (Thucydides) 
2. π. ἀρὰν εἰς παῖδα to draw aside  the curse on thy son's head, (Euripides) 
3. Mid. to draw over to one's own side, seduce, detach , (Xenophon Historicus) 
4. to take away , (Xenophon Historicus): Mid., παραιρεῖσθαι τὴν θρασύτητα to lessen, damp  it, (Demosthenes Orator) 
5. generally, to take away from, steal away from , τί τινος (Herdotus Historicus) (ML)
